goal.objective: Close Candidate 3 release-proof gaps by running the canonical gate from exact committed candidate sources and making the R2 security context source-complete.

assumptions.statement: ADR 0005 and the Specialist Run contract are the complete implementation authority.
assumptions.rationale: Architecture fan-in passed and no blocking decision remains.

assumptions.statement: Candidate 3 is retired because its tracked-only gate receipt cannot prove that untracked verification inputs were absent.
assumptions.rationale: The exact product/API/IDE R2 handoff returned fix even though the functional gate passed.

assumptions.statement: The canonical command must execute repository inputs materialized exclusively from the requested candidate Git tree; installed dependencies remain external runtime supply and must not be represented as candidate source.
assumptions.rationale: A committed-source materialization closes the finding without maintaining an incomplete allowlist of potentially influential untracked paths.

assumptions.statement: After native apply_patch fails before mutation, the host may perform an exact precondition-hash-guarded PowerShell write inside the approved scope.
assumptions.rationale: Foundation Revision 1 proved the temporary worktree patch helper is unavailable while bounded escalated PowerShell writes remain auditable and fail closed.

assumptions.statement: All runtime, persistence, Git, merge, and memory effects remain external to core.
assumptions.rationale: V12 is a pure evidence session, not the deferred universal scheduler.

assumptions.statement: R2 security review must bind .gitattributes, .gitignore, src/specialist-handoff-schema-data.ts, src/specialist-handoff-schema.ts, src/specialist-schema-data.ts, and src/specialist-schema.ts as exact candidate snapshots.
assumptions.rationale: The independent security review identified those six causal correction files as missing from its approved context.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Prove the canonical command used only exact committed candidate source bytes and give R2 every primary source required to audit the causal gate and schema corrections.

evidenceRequirements.description: Prove an untracked source or test cannot influence the canonical command, bind candidate commit/tree/materialization identity and exact gate outputs, prove R2 includes all six security-critical candidate snapshots, and report focused syntax, format, test, and fail-closed results.

contextSources.description: Accepted immutable Specialist Run Session decision.
Exact Candidate 3 canonical-gate receipt whose tracked-only identity proof was rejected.
Exact Candidate 3 product/API/IDE FIX handoff identifying the untracked-input proof gap.
Repository text and binary normalization policy for evidence byte preservation.
Repository ignore policy that currently suppresses canonical-gate raw log evidence.
Existing Specialist Handoff schema validator pattern.
Revision-3 package-owned common and Specialist Handoff schema data.
Existing package scripts and export map.
Exact owner approval for the revision-8 package.
Exact revision-8 candidate-evidence byte-integrity PASS handoff.
Owner-approved revision-8 byte-integrity package.
Complete revision-8 PASS handoff report.
Current candidate-bound canonical gate wrapper.
Current candidate-bound R2 release-review preparation and compilation harness.
Current exact R2 reviewer handoff verifier.
Frozen normative Specialist Run contract.
Existing Specialist Compiler schema validator pattern.
Package-owned Specialist Compiler schema data used without runtime filesystem reads.
Frozen V12 verification obligations.
Frozen V12 feature specification.

forbiddenEffects: Do not use network access, change Git state, launch descendants, write outside the exact scope, or claim that core performs host effects.

workUnits.objective: Prove the canonical command used only exact committed candidate source bytes and give R2 every primary source required to audit the causal gate and schema corrections.

module.action: Materialize and authenticate the exact candidate Git tree before running the canonical command, close and validate the stronger receipt in R2, bind the six omitted security sources, and add focused adversarial regression coverage.

stopConditions: If native apply_patch fails before mutation because of the host sandbox, an exact precondition-hash-guarded PowerShell write is allowed within the declared write scope; verify the resulting file bytes immediately.
Return only the concrete closed SpecialistAgentHandoff JSON shape from the generated contract.
Run only local focused verification allowed by the contract and report every failing command truthfully.
Stop if any declared source is unavailable or fails its exact digest and byte binding.
Write only the exact declared write scope; do not change Git state, use the network, launch descendants, or widen the public contract.
